methodology: Compared declared class, interface, and enum member names from both Deno documentation graphs.
Compared exact structural types for the Tier A option/data contracts and representative Tier C management contracts.
Compared key sets instead of direct equality where otherwise-identical interfaces return package-nominal class instances.
Compiled the defaulted call forms against both packages and asserted mutable property shapes exactly.
Reviewed inherited members and std/fs re-export inheritance to eliminate false positives from declared-only member lists.

mismatches.upstream: spawn(command: string | URL, options?: SpawnOptions): Promise<ChildProcess>
mismatches.limabox: spawn(command: string | URL, options: SpawnOptions): Promise<ChildProcess>
mismatches.impact: Upstream-valid sandbox.spawn("cmd") calls do not compile against Limabox.
mismatches.resolution: Options are optional on the abstract consumer contract.

mismatches.impact: The upstream zero-argument and path-only call forms do not compile.
mismatches.resolution: Path and options are optional on the abstract consumer contract; providers own the upstream defaults.

mismatches.impact: The upstream deploy(app) form does not compile, even though this is a Tier C runtime stub.
mismatches.resolution: Options now default to an empty object before the explicit Tier C rejection.

mismatches.impact: Exact structural equality differs; ordinary command use remains source-compatible.
mismatches.resolution: The sh function property is mutable.

mismatches.upstream: mutable fields on ApiError, ConnectionEstablishmentError, ConnectionClosedError, RpcError, and SandboxKillError
mismatches.impact: Reads are compatible, but mutation accepted by upstream types is rejected by Limabox.
mismatches.resolution: All affected diagnostics now expose the same mutable and optional/undefined property shapes.

mismatches.impact: Node inspection output and exact public member keysets differ; execution behavior is unaffected.
mismatches.resolution: All four abstract value contracts implement the upstream Node custom-inspection symbol and shape.

internalConstructorExclusions.reason: Upstream exposes runtime-token constructors in documentation but tags them @internal; normal consumers cannot construct a valid instance. Limabox uses abstract provider-backed contracts instead.
